new_classes.label_en: semi-sparkling wine of fresh grapes with PDO, actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol, other, in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2204 22 38)
new_classes.label_de: Perlwein aus frischen Weintrauben mit g.U., vorhandener Alkoholgehalt ≤ 15% vol, anderer, in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2204 22 38)
new_classes.definition_en: Semi-sparkling wine (pétillant/frizzante) made from fresh grapes, bearing a protected designation of origin (PDO) as defined in additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, with an actual alcoholic strength not exceeding 15% vol, covering varieties not separately enumerated (other), presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres. Classified under CN subheading 2204 22 38.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 22 38: (1) semi-sparkling wine character (via parent class SemiSparklingWineWithPDO2204Small ⊂ SemiSparklingWine), (2) PDO status (via someValuesFrom ProtectedDesignationOfOriginQuality), (3) container volume ≤ 2 L (via someValuesFrom SmallContainer), and (4) alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol (via decimalRange maxInclusive 15.0 on alcoholByVolumePercent). The 'other' qualifier — meaning varieties not separately enumerated under 2204 22 22–26 (e.g. Tokaj, and the >15% vol PDO sub-codes) — cannot be cleanly expressed in OWL without enumerating all sibling subheadings and using complement axioms, which would risk inconsistency in the current TBox context. That sibling-exclusion aspect is therefore not formally captured, reducing coverage to ~0.72. Additionally, the precise scope of 'additional note 6(a)' defining PDO for wine is referenced but not axiomatised here, as it is already represented by the existing ProtectedDesignationOfOriginQuality class.
